checks.a_analysis: The document content was cross-referenced slide-by-slide between the source and the output JSON. Each slide's text content appears exactly as many times in the output as it does in the source. There are no repeated paragraphs or sections that would indicate a duplication error.

The heading hierarchy is logical and follows a consistent structure. Slide titles are assigned H1 (level 1). Sub-headings within complex slides, such as those on Page 24 ('Inclusive meeting tips') and Page 25 ('Microsoft Learn'), are correctly assigned H2 (level 2). There are no instances of skipped levels (e.g., H1 to H3) or incorrect nesting.

The tabular representations on Page 3 (Statistics) and Page 8 (Copilot features) correctly capture the structure of the source material. Page 3 is converted into a 4x2 table with headers for the numbers and data for the descriptions. Page 8 correctly identifies the categories as headers in a 4-column layout. The spans and row/column organization match the visual intent of the source.

The reading order for elements matches the slide-by-slide sequence of the source. Within individual slides, the order follows a top-to-bottom and left-to-right logic. Although content is missing on Page 25, the remaining heading and image elements are sequenced correctly in relation to each other.

While the lists on Page 24 are converted correctly, the lists on Page 25 are completely missing from the output. Slide 25 in the source contains lists under 'Microsoft Learn', 'Accessibility links', and 'LinkedIn Learning'. The JSON output for this section includes the H2 headings but excludes the actual list elements and their items.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 25 contains lists under three subheadings: 'Microsoft Learn' (4 items), 'Accessibility links' (3 items), and 'LinkedIn Learning' (1 item).
checks.b_evidence_output: In the JSON section for Page 25, the elements list only contains the H1 title, H2 headings, and an image, with no list elements present between reading_order 205 and 210, 210 and 214, or 214 and 216.

checks.a_analysis: Several links clearly visible in the source PDF on Page 25 (e.g., 'Accessibility Fundamentals', 'Accessibility Technology & Tools | Microsoft Accessibility') are entirely missing from the output content. The text and associated navigation structure for these links were not captured during conversion.
checks.b_evidence_source: Slide 25 contains multiple links such as 'Accessibility Fundamentals' and 'Accessibility Technology & Tools | Microsoft Accessibility'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The text for these links is missing from the element list for the section corresponding to Page 25.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML conversion successfully transforms the multi-column and grid-based layouts of the PowerPoint slides into a logical, single-column linear flow suitable for web consumption. For instance, the statistics on slide 3 and the feature grid on slide 8 are converted into readable tables. The lists of tools on slides 5 and 6 are organized under clear headings. The visual organization of information remains consistent with the source's intent.

The tables present in the HTML output, such as those representing the data from slides 3 and 8, are rendered correctly with visible structure and aligned content. They are easily readable and correctly associate headers with data cells.

Meaningful images from the source PDF are missing throughout the HTML output. This includes the Microsoft logo on the title page (represented only by text), illustrative screenshots of the 'Accessibility Assistant' on slide 10, the photo of a man and dog on slide 11, the video screenshot on slide 13, the status icons on slide 14, the 'Clippy' GIF on slide 16, and the SharePoint screenshots on slide 17. These images are essential for demonstrating the visual accessibility features discussed in the presentation.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains meaningful images on slides 1 (logo), 10 (3 screenshots), 11 (photo), 13 (video screenshot), 14 (3 icons), 16 (GIF), and 17 (3 screenshots).
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML output is missing these images. In several sections, such as 'Convey information' and 'Emojis and GIFs', the space where images should be is either empty or the text continues without the visual context. The Microsoft logo appears only as a text placeholder 'Microsoft corporate logo'.
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the document, specifically corresponding to slides 1, 10, 11, 13, 14, 16, and 17.

checks.a_analysis: There is no evidence of visual content duplication within the body of the HTML output. Paragraphs, lists, and tables appear only once in their respective sections. The appearance of headings in the Table of Contents at the start of the document is an expected navigation feature and not considered content duplication.
